title: Parties taxonomy (canonical hand-authored roster)
description: Hand-authored canonical party roster at taxonomy/parties.json. Phase 1.1 of TODO/20260517-canonical-long-format-pivot.md. Replaces the pre-pivot parties-master.schema.json (which lived under datasets/elections/<event>/<state>/parties.json shards). Each party_id is yen-gov-issued (parties.IN.<SHORT_SLUG>) because ECI codes mutate and are not citizen-readable. ECI codes are kept as aliases (a list — ECI reassigns them over time). The compiled Parquet form is datasets/taxonomy/parties.parquet, joined into observations on (party_id <= entity_id_party_rollup). See canonical-store.md §3a.

x-changelog.description: Initial canonical party roster schema. Per canonical-store.md §3a (D-elections, Phase 1.1) + Hans + Max + user 2026-05-18. party_id = parties.IN.<SHORT_SLUG>, hand-authored. aliases capture name variants (AIADMK vs ADMK vs A.I.A.D.M.K.). eci_codes is a list because ECI reassigns codes over time. predecessor_of / successor_of capture splits and mergers (DMK -> MDMK split; Janata Dal lineage chain). Independent collapses to parties.IN.IND; NOTA to parties.IN.NOTA.

BREAKING (PR-T row 1.10, T-2 — Hans + Max Q2 verdict 2026-05-19): rename party-lineage FK fields to the `_party_id` FK-suffix convention for consistency with the rest of the canonical store (entity_id_party_rollup, party_id on every observation row). The rename is value-preserving but CROSSED at the key level — a naive sed-style `s/successor_of/successor_party_id/` would INVERT every lineage pointer's direction.

Mapping (value-preserving, key-crossed):
  old `predecessor_of: X` (the holder is the predecessor; X is the successor) -> new `successor_party_id: X` (X is the successor)
  old `successor_of: X`   (the holder is the successor; X is the predecessor) -> new `predecessor_party_id: X` (X is the predecessor)

This is why the bump is major, not minor: at the JSON-key layer the old field names are gone (forbidden by additionalProperties:false), and the new keys carry semantics that swap which side of the relationship they describe. Any external consumer reading `predecessor_of` expecting it to point to the predecessor would silently get the successor under the new shape — which is the OPPOSITE of what the consumer would expect from the literal field name. Confidence that this is safe to ship without a deprecation window: no production code in this repo reads either field (verified via grep across backend/yen_gov/**/*.py at commit-time — the canonical party_lookup loader uses party_id/short_name/full_name/aliases/eci_codes/alliance_history but never lineage). The lineage fields are forward-looking metadata sitting in parties.json for a future frontend lineage walker; the frontend has no consumer today. External consumers (datasets/taxonomy/parties.json is fetched as a static asset by the frontend) inherit the new shape on the next deploy; no backwards-compat shim ships because there are no readers to shim.

One-shot migration: tools/_oneshot_parties_v1_to_v2.py walks parties.json, renames keys preserving values (crossed mapping above), and bumps $schema_version 1.0 -> 2.0. Idempotent only on a v1.0 input — do not re-run on a v2.0 file. Deleted in the same commit.

Test-fixture update: backend/tests/test_canonical_eci_backfill.py carries 7 PartyDimRow fixture rows each holding the old field names; same crossed rename applied. Companion commit on indicator-schema (PR-T row 1.10 / T-1) v4.3 -> v4.4 is additive, not breaking; only this T-2 commit is the breaking bump in PR-T.

Field descriptions clarified: `successor_party_id` (was `predecessor_of`) explicitly says 'this party's successor'; `predecessor_party_id` (was `successor_of`) explicitly says 'this party's predecessor'. The new names match the citizen-readable English literal reading.

Additive (PR-R.2 / no-UNK-regression): no schema shape change. Marker bump to reflect that the canonical roster at datasets/taxonomy/parties.json has been expanded from 32 -> 108 entries by folding in datasets/reference/in/parties.json (Wikipedia + ECI 76 entries) and hand-curating the top unresolved party_shorts surfaced by the party-taxonomy gap report (.runtime/_party_gap_report.json: top 60 covered ~83% of unresolved candidates). Same field set as 2.0; only the data grew. The legacy overlay at datasets/reference/in/parties.json + parties-discovered.json (plus the matching parties-master.schema.json + parties-discovered.schema.json) was subsequently retired in PR-R.3 (row 1.8e, 2026-05-19): backend/yen_gov/pipeline/compose.load_eci_party_registry() now reads this taxonomy file as the single source of truth, the auto-extending discovered overlay path was deleted, and the frontend's parties-registry fetch was removed. The companion structural fix is the additive party_short_raw column on dim_candidates (its own schema v1.0 -> v1.1) so the UI shows the upstream short_name even when party_lookup still falls through to parties.IN.UNK.

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12. Hand-authored taxonomy: empty array OR a single entry naming the editorial-judgement source (e.g. ECI's recognised-party list).

party_id.description: PK. yen-gov slug: parties.IN.<SHORT_SLUG> (e.g. parties.IN.DMK, parties.IN.BJP, parties.IN.IND, parties.IN.NOTA). Hand-authored, stable across time. The id is yen-gov's; aliases / eci_codes are how upstream rows resolve to it.

short_name.description: Canonical abbreviation (DMK, AIADMK, BJP). Display label.

full_name.description: Full legal/registered name. Display label on detail pages.

aliases.description: Alternative short_names that resolve to this row during ECI ingest lookup (e.g. AIADMK <-> ADMK <-> A.I.A.D.M.K.).

eci_codes.description: List of ECI numeric party codes ever observed for this party. ECI reassigns codes over time, so this is a list, not a single value. Empty for Independent/NOTA which carry no ECI code.

state_scope.description: Where the party is politically active. 'IN' = national; '[SU]\d{2}' = ECI state code. National parties carry ['IN']. State parties carry their state codes (DMK -> ['S22']). Pan-state regional parties carry multiple.

dissolved_year.description: Year dissolved / merged / deregistered. Null if active.

successor_party_id.description: v2.0 (was `predecessor_of` in v1.0; CROSSED rename per PR-T row 1.10 / T-2 Hans + Max Q2 verdict 2026-05-19). FK to THIS party's successor party_id. Example: Bharatiya Jana Sangh.successor_party_id = parties.IN.BJP (BJS became BJP via the Janata Party interregnum). Captures splits and mergers — the frontend lineage walker traverses this forward.

predecessor_party_id.description: v2.0 (was `successor_of` in v1.0; CROSSED rename per PR-T row 1.10 / T-2). FK to THIS party's predecessor party_id. Example: parties.IN.BJP.predecessor_party_id = parties.IN.BJS (BJP came from BJS). Reverse pointer of successor_party_id — the frontend lineage walker traverses this backward.

period_label.description: ECI event id (e.g. AcGenMay2026) the alliance was declared for.

alliance.description: Pre-poll alliance name (SPA, NDA, INDIA). Null for non-aligned that event.

alliance_history.description: Optional per-event alliance history. Lets a citizen track which front a party was on without scanning every per-event result file.

notes.description: Editorial note (e.g. 'Recognised state party in TN since 1973'). Citizen-facing on the party detail page.
